LAW AND MOTION, 302, ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: PLAINTIFF GLORIA WONG'S MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. ALTHOUGH THE OPPOSITION WAS LATE, THE COURT CONSIDERED IT AND DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE THE MOTION FOR A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION BECAUSE IT IS FAR FROM CLEAR THAT PLAINTIFF IS LIKELY TO PREVAIL ON THE MERITS GIVEN THE SUBMISSION OF THE TWO DIFFERENT CONTRACTS FOR THE PURCHASE OF THE BUSINESS. AS 10 PERCENT OWNERS OF THE PROPERTY, DEFENDANTS APPEAR TO HAVE A RIGHT TO BE ON THE PROPERTY. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR # 8600

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Apr-11-2007 Denied
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCE:
Gerard Launay, Esq., 510-486-1000 and Siu Ma, Esq., 415-566-5300 for plaintiff and moving party Gloia Wong
Randall Choy, Esq. of Hedani, Choy, Spalding & Salvagione, 415-778-0800 for defendant and opposing party Suzanne Hoo
George Leal, Esq. of Chan, Doi & Leal, 415-281-8988 for defendant and opposing party Darren Lee

RULING:
ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING.
PLAINTIFF GLORIA WONG'S Motion For Preliminary Injunction. ALTHOUGH THE OPPOSITION WAS LATE, THE COURT CONSIDERED IT AND DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE THE MOTION FOR A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION BECAUSE IT IS FAR FROM CLEAR THAT PLAINTIFF IS LIKELY TO PREVAIL ON THE MERITS GIVEN THE SUBMISSION OF THE TWO DIFFERENT CONTRACTS FOR THE PURCHASE OF THE BUSINESS. AS 10 PERCENT OWNERS OF THE PROPERTY, DEFENDANTS APPEAR TO HAVE A RIGHT TO BE ON THE PROPERTY. (302/PJM/PB) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR # 8600
